title: Make pattern matches exhaustive over sealed types
description: Match on sealed traits/enums so the compiler enforces every case, turning missing branches into compile errors not MatchErrors.
body: Use when branching on an algebraic data type (a sealed trait with case class/object children or a Scala 3 enum). Do not add a catch-all _ case that hides new variants or leave a match the compiler warns is non-exhaustive. Actions: declare the hierarchy sealed so the compiler knows all subtypes, match each case explicitly, and treat the non-exhaustiveness warning as an error (-Wconf or -Xfatal-warnings). Validation signals include a clean compile with no exhaustiveness warning and a test covering each variant. Risk boundary: a swallowed non-exhaustive warning becomes a runtime MatchError when a new variant is added later; a blanket _ on a domain ADT silently mishandles future cases and should be replaced with explicit branches.
